body.title: Decision rollup preserves original narrative (don't overwrite)
body.result: cc_queue.py:_decision_rollup now preserves existing narrative (appends ## Rollup section, flips status open→closed) or writes stub form if no narrative. 3 overwritten docs restored from git. 2 test scripts verify preserve + stub paths. Commit c6f388bb.
body.description:
Decision rollup at cc_queue.py:_decision_rollup (line 360) currently calls write_text() which wipes the original narrative decision doc. All three decisions closed so far (D-worker-mode-routing-2026-04-21, D-cc-workflow-optimizations-2026-04-21, D-scaffold-not-correct-2026-04-21) lost their design narratives — only the Closed-at + Shipped-via summary survives, making retrospective context recovery depend on git history.

Proposed: detect existing narrative (content not starting with `# Decision rollup —`). If present: append `## Rollup` section at end + flip frontmatter `status: open` → `status: closed`. If absent (decision had no narrative): keep current stub form.

Also restore the 3 already-overwritten docs from git history (git show <originating-commit>:<path> for each).

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (_decision_rollup function), lab/design_docs/decisions/D-worker-mode-routing-2026-04-21.md (restore), lab/design_docs/decisions/D-cc-workflow-optimizations-2026-04-21.md (restore), lab/design_docs/decisions/D-scaffold-not-correct-2026-04-21.md (restore)

**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not (applies — plan below); docs-in-code (doesn't apply — glue code, not load-bearing subsystem); no-sqlite / oop-first / no-new-memory-schemas (don't apply)

**Scope boundary:** In: _decision_rollup function logic + 3 doc restorations. Out: decision_manager.py, palace decision nodes, other cc_queue.py commands, /decided skill (narrative source is fine — it's the rollup that overwrites), T-decisions-into-palace-subtree (separate follow-up).

**Test plan:** (a) apply fix to cc_queue.py; (b) restore 3 docs from git + append Rollup section using the new shape; (c) read each restored doc and confirm narrative + rollup both present; (d) unit-test _decision_rollup directly: call with a stubbed tasks list against a test file containing narrative, confirm narrative preserved.
